rules: The report may include producer-specific route or destination references, but the semantic envelope stays stable across surfaces.
A report with blocking failed checks cannot claim overall_state=ready.
route_snapshot is expected for paired surface readiness bundles.
Blocking failed checks should include either a concrete next_step or a corresponding top-level next_steps entry.

detail_lines, repair_destination_ref, project_context_summary, hub_memory_prompt_projection, project_governance_runtime_readiness, project_memory_policy, project_memory_readiness, project_memory_assembly_resolution, supervisor_memory_policy, supervisor_memory_assembly_resolution, supervisor_review_trigger_snapshot, project_remote_snapshot_cache_snapshot, supervisor_remote_snapshot_cache_snapshot, heartbeat_governance_snapshot, memory_route_truth_snapshot, durable_candidate_mirror_snapshot, local_store_write_snapshot, and skill_doctor_truth_snapshot are optional producer context and do not change the normalized check semantics.
project_context_summary is currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose a human-readable projection of project context assembly truth alongside the raw detail lines.

hub_memory_prompt_projection is currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose Hub-provided prompt assembly counts/source kinds as first-class machine-readable explainability without reparsing detail_lines or locally inferring prompt content.
project_governance_runtime_readiness is currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose the A-tier configured-vs-runtime-ready split as first-class machine-readable diagnostics.

project_memory_policy, project_memory_readiness, and project_memory_assembly_resolution are currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose Project AI role-aware memory policy, readiness, and effective assembly truth as first-class machine-readable diagnostics.

supervisor_memory_policy and supervisor_memory_assembly_resolution are currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose Supervisor role-aware review-memory policy and effective assembly truth as first-class machine-readable diagnostics.

supervisor_review_trigger_snapshot is currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose Supervisor review policy mode, effective trigger set, active heartbeat-derived review candidate, queue posture, and latest review-note provenance as first-class machine-readable diagnostics.

supervisor_guidance_continuity_snapshot is currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose Supervisor latest-review/latest-guidance continuity, ack posture, and safe-point posture as first-class machine-readable diagnostics.

supervisor_safe_point_timeline_snapshot is currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose pending guidance delivery timing, live safe-point posture, and tool-batch pause posture as first-class machine-readable diagnostics.

project_remote_snapshot_cache_snapshot and supervisor_remote_snapshot_cache_snapshot are read-only XT projections of remote snapshot cache provenance; they must not be treated as durable truth or as permission to bypass Hub-first routing.

heartbeat_governance_snapshot is currently used by XT session runtime readiness to expose project heartbeat quality, anomalies, effective cadence, next-review-due truth, and attached project-memory-readiness explainability alongside the raw detail lines.

skill_doctor_truth_snapshot is currently used by XT skills compatibility readiness to expose the project effective skill profile plus typed per-skill readiness counts/previews as first-class machine-readable doctor truth.
hub_memory_prompt_projection is a read-only XT carrier of Hub prompt-assembly explainability and must not be treated as prompt authority, export approval, route truth, or a second memory resolver.
memory_route_truth_snapshot is a read-only projection of upstream memory route diagnostics and must not be treated as a second route resolver.

durable_candidate_mirror_snapshot is a read-only XT projection of supervisor durable-candidate mirror state and must not be treated as durable promotion, canonical save success, or read cutover truth.
local_store_write_snapshot is a read-only XT projection of local cache/fallback/edit-buffer write provenance and must not be treated as durable writer authority.
Structured producer fields should be preferred over reparsing detail_lines when both are available.
This object is an optional XT-originated projection of project effective skill capability truth and per-skill typed readiness truth.
It exists so generic doctor consumers can see runnable/grant-required/approval-required/blocked counts and representative governed skills without reparsing free-form detail_lines.
It is read-only explainability and must not be treated as a grant token, runtime bypass, or a replacement for Supervisor preflight / tool runtime deny.
This object is an optional XT-originated projection of Supervisor review/guidance continuity across latest review note, latest guidance, and any pending-ack guidance.
It keeps ack posture, delivery mode, intervention mode, safe-point policy, rendered refs, and summaryLine machine-readable for generic doctor consumers.

It is read-only explainability only and must not be treated as review-note authority, guidance-injection authority, interrupt authority, grant truth, or a bypass around Hub scheduler, audit, TTL, kill-switch, or clamp.
This object is an optional XT-originated projection of Supervisor review policy posture and the current heartbeat-derived review trigger candidate.
It keeps effective trigger sets, candidate review level/run kind, queue posture, latest review-note provenance, and summaryLine machine-readable for generic doctor consumers.

It is read-only review-engine explainability only and must not be treated as scheduler authority, review-note authority, guidance-injection authority, grant truth, or a bypass around Hub audit, TTL, kill-switch, clamp, or policy gates.
This object is an optional XT-originated projection of pending Supervisor guidance delivery timing inside the current Project AI safe-point loop.
It keeps pending guidance posture, live flow location, prompt visibility, delivery state, and tool-batch pause posture machine-readable for generic doctor consumers.
It is read-only explainability only and must not be treated as scheduler authority, interrupt authority, grant truth, or a bypass around Hub audit, TTL, kill-switch, clamp, or policy gates.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes remote snapshot cache provenance for either Project AI or Supervisor memory assembly.
It keeps cache scope, cache age, and TTL-left machine-readable so doctor/export consumers do not have to infer cache freshness from free-form detail_lines.
It is explainability only and must not be treated as durable memory source truth, grant truth, export approval, or a way to bypass fail-closed boundaries.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it is a presentation-friendly projection of raw project context diagnostics.
It must not be treated as grant truth, policy truth, or a replacement for the normalized check state.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it carries Hub-provided prompt assembly explainability for the latest governed coding turn.
It must directly reuse the upstream Hub Generate.done metadata rather than locally inferring prompt contents from XT detail_lines.

It is read-only explainability only and must not be treated as prompt authority, grant truth, export approval, route truth, or a bypass around constitution, audit, TTL, kill-switch, or remote export gates.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes the A-tier configured-vs-runtime-ready split as machine-readable diagnostics.
It keeps configured/effective execution tier, runtime surface posture, trusted automation state, and missing readiness reasons visible without reparsing detail_lines.
It must not be treated as Hub grant truth, tool-policy authority, or a bypass around TTL, kill-switch, clamp, or audit chains.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes Project AI role-aware memory policy truth as machine-readable diagnostics.
It keeps configured/recommended/effective project dialogue and context depth plus the A-tier memory ceiling visible without reparsing detail_lines.
It must not be treated as grant truth, runtime authority, or a replacement for hard allow/deny boundaries such as Hub grant, runtime readiness, tool policy, TTL, or kill-switch.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes XT-observed Project AI memory assembly readiness as machine-readable diagnostics.
It exists so API / export / operator surfaces can consume continuity-floor and projection-drift signals without reparsing detail_lines.
It must not be treated as durable memory truth, scheduler control, grant approval, or a bypass around Hub-first memory governance.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes the effective role-aware memory assembly resolution as machine-readable diagnostics.
selected_slots, selected_planes, selected_serving_objects, and excluded_blocks exist so consumers do not have to infer effective memory assembly from free-form detail_lines.
It must not be treated as execution authority, grant approval, or scheduler control.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes Supervisor role-aware review-memory policy truth as machine-readable diagnostics.
It keeps configured/recommended/effective Recent Raw Context and Review Memory Depth plus the S-tier review-memory ceiling visible without reparsing detail_lines.
It must not be treated as extra execution permission or as a replacement for Hub grant/policy/clamp/TTL boundaries.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes the cadence triple plus due explainability for a single heartbeat/review dimension.
It must not be treated as a scheduler authority surface or as permission to bypass governance review safe points.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it exposes the next governance review due state as explainability only.
It must not be upgraded into autonomous intervention authority or release-readiness proof.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it mirrors XT heartbeat-governed review explainability on session runtime readiness.
Its semantics are diagnostic only and must not be treated as memory assembly authority, project execution truth authority, or policy/grant truth.
The snapshot should preserve the configured/recommended/effective cadence triple and next-review-due semantics when present.
Digest visibility / reason codes and digest three-line text should remain visible when producer projections explain why a user digest was shown or suppressed.
Attached project-memory readiness fields remain explainability-only and must not be misread as memory assembly authority.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it mirrors XT recovery-beat explainability on session runtime readiness.
It remains diagnostic-only and must not be upgraded into runtime restart authority, route mutation authority, or grant/policy override authority.
queued_review_* fields describe follow-up governance intent only and do not themselves authorize intervention.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it should directly reuse the Diagnostic-First Route Surface frozen by the memory model routing contract.
Doctor, diagnostics, and export surfaces should display the same route truth semantics when this object is present.
Producer-local clamp or serving facts must be expressed separately and must not overwrite route_result semantics.
projection_source identifies which frozen diagnostics source produced the snapshot, while completeness declares whether the payload is full upstream truth or an explicit partial XT projection.
If the producer only has partial XT route facts, missing fields must be emitted as explicit unknown values rather than omitted or silently fabricated.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it mirrors XT session-runtime evidence about supervisor durable-candidate handoff.
Its semantics are diagnostic only and must not be upgraded into canonical Hub memory truth, durable promotion success, or read-source authority.
This object is optional and producer-specific; it mirrors XT session-runtime evidence about which local cache/fallback/edit-buffer write path most recently touched each store surface.
Its semantics are diagnostic only and must not be upgraded into durable writer truth, canonical Hub save success, or read-source authority.
Each next step must be concrete enough to guide repair, retry, or escalation.
destination_ref may point to a producer-specific UI surface, CLI subcommand, or recovery entrypoint.
Route snapshot captures current transport truth for paired surfaces and should not be silently omitted when the bundle depends on route health.
A request should not change readiness truth; it only selects the diagnostic bundle and verbosity.
Different surfaces should be able to request the same bundle and receive the same semantic shape.
